scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All seven pass criteria met: (1) Throughput gap clearly identified as 300/min inbound vs 15-20/min processing. (2) Unbounded backlog consequence described with specific math (~16,800 unprocessed after 1 hour, eventual silent loss via SQS retention expiry). (3) Visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with precise reasoning (10×3s=30s matching 30s timeout). (4) Missing DLQ identified as a blocker with infinite retry consequence. (5) Queue depth metric gap called out as CRITICAL. (6) Autoscaling recommended with specific sizing (minimum 15-20 worker instances). (7) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough throughput math including projection of 16,800 job backlog after 1 hour and silent data loss via SQS retention expiry
Visibility timeout analysis is precise — identifies the exact race condition where batch processing time matches the timeout
DLQ absence correctly framed as a blocker that amplifies the backlog problem, not just a best practice gap
Queue depth metric gap identified with clear operational consequence — backlog is invisible to on-call
Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional valuable findings: memory exhaustion risk, missing circuit breakers, per-message deletion recommendation, idempotency requirements
Confidence section is well-calibrated — clearly separates arithmetic certainties from areas requiring more information
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and actionable reasoning
